snapshot: U.S. cash equities fell on March 24, but risk appetite improved again after the close as ceasefire headlines lifted U.S. futures and knocked oil lower.
The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 53,030 and TOPIX futures ended at 3,607, leaving the Nikkei roughly 778 yen above the last cash close.
USD/JPY finished New York at 158.98, a pullback from the 159-yen area that eases some of the worst 'bad yen' pressure seen earlier.
WTI fell to $88.86 at 08:05 JST in Asian trade. The drop from the prior settlement of $92.35 is a relief input for Tokyo risk assets.
Reuters' expected Nikkei range is 52,500-53,100. A relief opening looks likely, but a gap-and-fade risk still remains if oil firms again.

drivers.driver: Ceasefire headlines and softer front-end oil
drivers.why_today: Reports that Washington sent Tehran a 15-point peace plan pushed WTI down into the high $80s. That sets up a futures-led relief rally in Tokyo.
USD/JPY retraced from the 159s to 158.98. That modestly reduces import-cost pressure and gives domestic-facing names a little breathing room.

drivers.driver: After-close IR, MBO and capital-policy news
drivers.why_today: Tokio Marine, Solasto, Niterra and Senshu Ikeda all released after-close disclosures that are easy to explain and likely to matter right from the opening print.

headline_inventory.headline: The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 53,030 and TOPIX futures ended at 3,607
Reuters' New York market summary showed USD/JPY at 158.98, the U.S. 10-year around 4.33%, and another decline in U.S. equities on March 24
WTI fell to $88.86 at 08:05 JST as the market priced a lower risk of prolonged supply disruption
Reuters expects the Nikkei to extend gains on March 25, with a projected 52,500-53,100 range
Tokio Marine announced a strategic partnership with Berkshire's insurance group, including a 2.49% stake sale and a simultaneous buyback
Nippon Sheet Glass said the privatization report is broadly factual and that its board is scheduled to decide on March 24
Niterra revised up both its full-year net-profit outlook and dividend forecast
Cosmos Initia revised up both its FY outlook and dividend plan
Solasto announced an MBO and recommended tendering into the offer
Senshu Ikeda Holdings revised up both earnings guidance and its year-end dividend forecast
Stella Chemifa raised full-year guidance and added a 110th-anniversary dividend
note announced a third-party allotment to KADOKAWA and a capital-and-business alliance

watchlist.name: Tokio Marine Holdings
watchlist.why_today: A Berkshire insurance unit is taking a stake, and the company paired the deal with a buyback to offset dilution. It is the clearest opening-check name on the board.
watchpoints: Can it hold the high-level indication after the opening auction
Does the market reward the buyback offset more than the dilution concern
Does buying stay concentrated in Tokio Marine rather than the insurance sector broadly
falsifiers: The market focuses first on dilution from the treasury-share disposal
The strong opening gap fades as headline freshness wears off
watchlist.red_team: As a mega-cap, the stock can lose momentum quickly after a strong gap-up.

watchlist.name: Solasto
watchlist.why_today: The company announced an MBO and recommended tendering into the offer. It is an event-driven name with a very high priority for opening-auction monitoring.
watchpoints: Does the stock move cleanly toward the event price from the opening bid
Does turnover stay alive even after the first match
Can the name avoid a sharp fade once the initial premium is reflected
falsifiers: Investors turn more cautious on the detailed tender conditions
Trading dries up quickly once the initial spread compression is done
watchlist.red_team: Once the tender spread is mostly closed, the upside can become mechanically capped.

ソラスト、下期配当を見送り

watchlist.name: Niterra
watchlist.why_today: It lifted full-year net profit and the annual dividend. This remains one of the cleaner large-cap earnings-and-return stories into today's open.
watchpoints: Can it carry over the strong initial reaction from the prior session
Are both the earnings revision and the dividend hike rewarded cleanly
Can the stock-specific story beat a generic export rebound
falsifiers: The market reduces the move to a simple FX-driven revision
Pure index-beta rebound trading drowns out the stock-specific catalyst
watchlist.red_team: A meaningful part of the upgrade still leans on external conditions, especially FX.

watchlist.name: Senshu Ikeda Holdings
watchlist.why_today: It raised both net profit and the year-end dividend after the close. The name adds a fresh numeric catalyst to the broader bank-rate theme.
watchpoints: Can it maintain relative strength inside the regional-bank group
Does the higher payout keep attracting early buying
Can volume stay alive after the opening print
falsifiers: The stock is swallowed by a generic regional-bank move
The prior afternoon reaction already priced in most of the news
watchlist.red_team: Its stock-specific edge can fade quickly if the bank theme turns too broad.

watchlist.name: Cosmos Initia
watchlist.why_today: It raised FY guidance and the dividend plan. After the recent pullback, the stock offers both rebound room and a cleaner yield case than many real-estate peers.
watchpoints: Can it hold a gain in the 10% area
Does the market keep rewarding the higher dividend yield
Can it outperform the broader real-estate group on its own disclosures
falsifiers: Investors focus too much on one-off profit elements
Liquidity on the Standard market proves too thin for follow-through
watchlist.red_team: The numbers are strong, but the one-off tint has not fully disappeared.

watchlist.name: Stella Chemifa
watchlist.why_today: It moved to an operating-profit growth plan and added an anniversary dividend. The setup combines a clean earnings revision with shareholder return support.
watchpoints: Can the prior session's acceleration carry into a second day
Does the market value the earnings improvement more than the anniversary-dividend angle
Can turnover stay active inside the materials bucket
falsifiers: The move is treated as a one-off dividend trade
Weakness in chemicals spills over and caps follow-through
watchlist.red_team: The story is good, but second-day continuation after a sharp reaction is never automatic.

watchlist.name: note
watchlist.why_today: It announced a third-party allotment to KADOKAWA alongside a capital-and-business alliance. The collaboration theme is strong, but dilution is part of the setup.
watchpoints: Does the cooperation story outweigh dilution concerns
Does a better Growth-market tone help the name
Can it hold a high opening level with real turnover
falsifiers: The market focuses first on the dilutive share issuance
The earnings impact is judged too distant
watchlist.red_team: The partnership angle is compelling, but the equity issuance can destabilize the initial reaction.

deep_dive.title: Tokio Marine: the key question is whether the buyback-offset logic can outlive the Buffett headline

lines: This is not just a generic Buffett-related headline. The more important point is how the market reads the combination of treasury-share disposal and the offsetting buyback.
As a liquid insurance mega-cap, Tokio Marine can attract both index-rebound money and stock-specific capital at the same time.

After the March 24 close, the company disclosed a strategic partnership with a Berkshire insurance subsidiary and a treasury-share disposal, while also approving a buyback intended to offset dilution.
Reuters confirmed a limit-up-level buy indication on March 24 morning, so the story still has second-day confirmation value into today's open.
Does the market reward the buyback offset more than it worries about dilution from the disposal.
Does turnover remain stock-specific rather than simply spreading across the insurance sector.
Dilution concerns dominate the first read and overwhelm the buyback logic.
The stock cannot sustain the opening premium and slips into a simple sector-sympathy trade.
If the broader rebound fades after the open, a mega-cap like Tokio Marine can still face quick profit-taking.
The cooperation path may take time to become tangible, leaving the stock exposed to headline fatigue.

no_trade_conditions: If Nikkei 225 futures fail to hold the 53,000 area before the cash open and the short-cover premise breaks
If WTI reclaims the low-90s decisively and the morning relief tone is undone by a fresh oil spike
If USD/JPY snaps back toward the upper 159s or intervention-style volatility suddenly returns
If the fresh after-close names such as Tokio Marine and Niterra fail to hold their opening premiums and the day turns into a pure index rebound only
content.disclaimer: This is a pre-open market digest, not investment advice. Please make your own final decisions.
